zh.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Witchingham於大約1376年出生在英國。他和Mrs Edward Witchingham至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Witchingham于大约1376年出生在英国。他和Mrs Edward Witchingham至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Witchingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Kitchenham
meanings: English: locative name from Kitchingham (in Etchingham, Sussex) or Kitchenham (in Peasmarsh, Sussex).

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Witchingham was born about 1376, in England. He had at least 1 daughter with Mrs Edward Witchingham.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Witchingham est né environ 1376, à Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Mrs Edward Witchingham.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Witchingham wurde um 1376 in England geboren. Er hatte mit Mrs Edward Witchingham mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Witchingham è nato il circa 1376 nel England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Mrs Edward Witchingham.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Witchingham(은)는 대략 1376년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 Mrs Edward Witchingham(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Witchingham nasceu aproximadamente 1376, em Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Mrs Edward Witchingham.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Witchingham родился около 1376 г. в местности Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Mrs Edward Witchingham.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Witchingham nació aproximadamente 1376, en Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Mrs Edward Witchingham.
